failure_surface: Agent failed to complete the task successfully (eval score 0) despite executing code without runtime errors. The output did not produce correct formula values.
root_cause: The agent made two critical assumptions without verification: (1) it assumed column I existed for formatting preservation when max_columns=8 confirmed only columns A-H exist, making the preservation logic pointless; (2) it guessed column mappings (Cost=C, ITV=E, Profit=F) without inspecting the actual header cell values to confirm their locations.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Before writing formulas or preserving styles, verify both the existence of target columns/rows AND confirm column-to-data mappings by reading actual header cell values rather than relying on visual CSV preview or guessing.
evidence: Inspection showed 'Max Columns: 8' but code still iterated over column 9 (I) for style preservation
All header inspections returned 'None' for columns A-G, suggesting agent guessed column mappings instead of using actual header names to locate Cost, ITV, Profit columns
No verification step checked if the identified formula result values were correct before saving

memory_items.title: Verify Column Existence Before Access
memory_items.lesson: Always check ws.max_column before accessing or modifying columns beyond the reported maximum to avoid referencing non-existent ranges.
memory_items.generalization: When preservation or multi-column operations are required, validate that each target column index <= ws.max_column before attempting access.

memory_items.title: Confirm Data Mapping via Header Values
memory_items.lesson: Do not guess column positions based on visual inspection alone; read actual header cell values and programmatically identify which column contains which field name.
memory_items.generalization: Map field names (Cost, ITV, Profit, etc.) to column indices by iterating through header row cells and matching string content, not positional assumptions.

memory_items.title: Validate Formula Results Before Finalizing
memory_items.lesson: After writing formulas, perform a spot-check by evaluating at least one resulting cell value against expected logic before saving the workbook.
memory_items.generalization: Include a post-write validation phase that verifies formula behavior matches intended business logic, especially for conditional calculations.

edits.content:
## 6. Verification Best Practices
- **Range Validation**: Always verify `ws.max_row` and `ws.max_column` before accessing ranges beyond these bounds.
- **Header-Based Mapping**: When formulas depend on specific data fields, read header cell values to determine correct column indices instead of guessing positions.
- **Post-Write Validation**: After writing formulas, spot-check computed values to confirm they reflect expected logical outcomes before finalizing the output file.
